Defendant Cummins Engine Company Sued Erroneously Herein As Cummins Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And Having Subject Matter Of Request For Admissions Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Brendan Way, Esq., of JACKSON, JENKINS & RENSTROM, LLP (415-982-3600), appearing for defendant / moving party Cummins Engine Company
RULING:
DEFENDANT CUMMINS ENGINE COMPANY (SUED ERRONEOUSLY HEREIN AS CUMMINS INC.)'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND HAVING SUBJECT MATTER OF REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS DEEMED ADMITTED AND FOR SANCTIONS
No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Cummins Engine Company's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories, special interrogatories and request for production of documents and having subject matter of request for admissions deemed admitted and for sanctions is granted. No opposition filed. Defendant is awarded sanctions of $1072.50. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)

MOTION For Order Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And Having Subject Matter Of Request For Admissions Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-09-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Cummins Engine Company's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories, special interrogatories and request for production and request for admissions deemed admitted and for sanctions is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to give proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. 1005(b). Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)
